headline: Malaysia-Turkey Defence Industry Partnership Signed
context: Malaysia and Turkey signed comprehensive defence cooperation in February 2026 covering drones armoured vehicles and naval systems.

cards.big: Malaysia-Turkey defence pact covers drones vehicles and naval systems
cards.sub: Agreement valued at RM3.5 billion over 10 years with technology transfer

cards.big: Turkey Baykar to establish drone maintenance facility for ASEAN region
cards.sub: Malaysia ordered 12 TB2 drones for maritime surveillance at RM280 million

cards.big: FNSS offered co-production of PARS vehicles at DRB-HICOM facilities
cards.sub: Joint venture would produce 150 vehicles with 65% local content for Army

cards.big: STM to provide technical assistance for littoral combat ship programme
cards.sub: Turkish MILGEM experience could address delayed LCS project

cards.big: Turkey partnership tests whether defence cooperation can accelerate stalled procurement programmes
Turkey defence partnership signed. Industrial cooperation must transfer technology, not just purchase equipment.
